creator: wbe@psr.com
text:
OK, so I tried it myself to verify the tarball and patches.

* mkdir /tmp/foo; cd /tmp/foo
* download the tarball and the 3 patch files from this bug report
* tar -xf newcalendar.tar.gz
* apply the 3 patch files with /usr/bin/patch: all changes successful

[local verification: cmp says the files now present match my current code.]

* cp $elsewhere/src.opts.mk /tmp/foo/
* make

make ran successfully for me, with no errors or warnings, up through producing the executable 'calendar' and calendar.1.gz.  make then stopped because neither the directory nor the file calendar/calendar.freebsd exist.  (I did not include those or the old/existing test/* test files in the tarball because they're all the same as the old code.  I only included the new test files and the new test/Makefile.)
